subject: Accounting
title: Adelphia's Family Affair
description: A quick, practical guide to Adelphia's Family Affair.

hook.text: The Rigas family founded Adelphia Communications—and then stole billions from it.

buildup.text: They used company funds for personal stock purchases, luxury condos, and a private golf course.

discovery.text: Adelphia hid $2.3 billion in debt by keeping it off the consolidated financial statements.

twist.text: When an analyst questioned the off-balance-sheet debt in 2002, the stock collapsed in days.

climax.text: John Rigas, the 79-year-old founder, was sentenced to 15 years in federal prison.

quiz.question: How did Adelphia hide its financial problems?
options: Keeping billions in debt off the financial statements
